user_story: As a user, I want to start a new project and specify its mode (such as 'Project Mode' or 'Trace' mode), so that I can organize my work according to my workflow needs.
true_user_story: The START NEW PROJECT function shall allow the user to create a new project.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to enter a new project name and the low level document path, so that I can create and configure a new project in RETRO efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name, low level document path, high level document path, and RTM file path, so that I can set up a new project with all necessary documentation.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name, low level document path, high level document path, and RTM file path, so that I can set up a new project with all required documentation.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select an IR Method from Vector-Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, or Keyword Extraction-IDF, so that I can choose the most suitable retrieval method for my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either 'Both Documents' or 'Low Level Document Only', with 'Both Documents' as the default, so that I can control the scope of keyword extraction.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a Weight Option (TF-IDF, Okapi, or LTU) when I choose Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF as my IR Method, so that I can customize how keywords are weighted in my search results.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide Weight Option from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a user, I want to be required to select a Weight Threshold when I choose the IR Method 'Keyword Extraction' or 'Keyword Extraction-IDF', so that the system can properly apply the selected method with the appropriate threshold.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF, the user must also select a Weight Threshold.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Thesaurus and Stop Word filename, so that I can customize my text processing according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Thesaurus Filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Thesaurus and Stop Word filename, so that I can customize text processing according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the Stop Word file.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select a Feedback Method, with "Standard Rochio" as the current option, so that I can provide feedback in a standardized way.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a Feedback Method.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the project creation process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created, so that I can easily back out if I change my mind or made a mistake.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a user, I want to create a project using the provided input fields and select a specific mode (such as Trace or Assess), so that the project is initialized in the desired mode for my workflow.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in “Trace” mode.

user_story: As a user, I want to load an existing project in its last saved state with all mode options, so that I can continue working on my project without losing progress.
true_user_story: Since the majority of projects are not completed in a single setting, then it is critical to provide the user the option to load an existing project to continue working on the project.  Load Project feature will provide the user the capacity to open an existing project in the last saved state with all the associated mode options. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a project name from a list of saved projects or enter a new project name, so that I can easily load or create projects in the system.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a project name from a list of saved projects.  Project names are obtained by scanning the saved projects directory.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Feedback Method, with 'Standard Rochio' as the current option, so that I can specify my preferred feedback approach during the process.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to select a feedback method.  The only option is “Standard Rochio”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the project loading or creation process at any time and return to the previous state, so that I can avoid making unwanted changes or continue my previous activity without committing to the action.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the LOAD PROJECT action and return to the previous activity.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel loading a project and return to my previous activity, so that I can avoid unwanted changes or interruptions if I change my mind.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load the project once the project name and feedback method is selected.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to use the LOAD RTM function to initialize a project with user-provided parameters, so that I can efficiently start projects with requirements traceability in place.
true_user_story: Similar in functionality to the START NEW PROJECT function, the LOAD RTM function collects project parameters from the user and initializes the project.  A project created via the LOAD RTM function may be in either “trace” or “assess” mode.  Assess mode is usually used when the user is provided a RTM and needs to evaluate its accuracy, completeness, and satisfaction level.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to enter a new project name and the low level document path, so that I can create and configure a new project efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the new project name.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name, low level document path, high level document path, and RTM file path, so that I can set up a new project with all necessary documentation.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the low level document path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the new project name, high level document path, low level document path, and RTM file path, so that I can set up a new project with all required documentation.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the high level document path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to provide the high-level document path and the RTM file path, so that RETRO can access the necessary files for processing requirements.
true_user_story: The user shall be prompted to provide the RTM file path.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to select an IR Method from Vector-Space Retrieval, Keyword Extraction, or Keyword Extraction-IDF, so that I can choose the most suitable retrieval method for my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select an IR Method from the following list: Vector-Space Retrieval Keyword Extraction Keyword Extraction-IDF

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either 'Both Documents' or 'Low Level Document Only', with 'Both Documents' as the default, so that I can control the scope of keyword extraction.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to select a Vocabulary Base of either “Both Documents” or “Low Level Document Only”.  The default value is “Both Documents”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select a Weight Option (TF-IDF, Okapi, or LTU) when I choose Keyword Extraction or Keyword Extraction-IDF as my IR Method, so that I can customize how keywords are weighted in my search results.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide “Weight Option” from the following list: TF-IDF (Default) Okapi LTU

user_story: As a user, I want to be required to select a Weight Threshold when I choose the IR Method of 'Keyword Extraction' or 'Keyword Extraction-IDF', so that the system can properly apply the selected method with the appropriate threshold.
true_user_story: If the user has selected an IR Method of “Keyword Extraction” or “Keyword Extraction-IDF”, the user must also select a “Weight Threshold”.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Thesaurus and Stop Word filename, so that I can customize my text processing according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Thesaurus” filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide Thesaurus and Stop Word filenames, so that I can customize the text processing according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Stop Word” filename.

user_story: As a user, I want to be optionally prompted to provide a Feedback Method, with 'Standard Rochio' as the current option, so that I can specify my preferred feedback approach during the process.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide a “Feedback Method”.  “Standard Rochio” is currently the only option.

user_story: As a user, I want to be prompted to select the Project Mode as either 'Trace' (default) or 'Assess', so that I can choose the appropriate mode for my workflow and accurately evaluate RTMs when needed.
true_user_story: The user shall optionally be prompted to provide the “Project Mode” of either “Trace” or “Assess”.  “Trace” is the default mode.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to cancel the project creation process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created, so that I can easily back out if I change my mind or made a mistake.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to cancel the process and return to the previous state at any time before the project is created.

user_story: As a user, I want to create a project using the provided input fields and select a mode (Project, Assess, or Trace), so that the project is initialized in the correct mode for my workflow.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create the project using the above input.  The project shall be placed in specified “Project Mode”.

user_story: As a user, I want to activate the SAVE function to save all current data and project status, so that I can preserve my work and continue later without losing progress.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to activate the SAVE function to save the current data and status of the project.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to prompt me to save my current project when I close it, so that I don't lose any unsaved work.
true_user_story: When activated RETRO will save all current data and project.

user_story: As a RETRO user, I want to close the current project from the File menu without exiting the application, so that I can work on other projects or tasks within RETRO efficiently.
true_user_story: The CLOSE CURRENT PROJECT Function will allow the user to close a given project without exiting the RETRO application.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to close the current project without exiting the RETRO application, so that I can switch between projects or pause my work without leaving the app.
true_user_story: The user shall be able close the current project.

user_story: As a RETRO user, I want to be able to exit the application when I am finished, so that I can close RETRO safely and efficiently.
true_user_story: It is necessary to provide a mechanism to exit the RETRO application when the user is finished working with the application.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to exit the RETRO application when I am finished, so that I can close the program easily and securely.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to exit the application.

user_story: As a user, I want to view the current project's XML file by selecting 'Show XML' from the Action menu, so that I can see a list of high and low connections in XML format on a new screen.
true_user_story: The user may want to view the current project’s XML file format.  The SHOW XML function will provide the user a display of the current XML file.

user_story: As a user, I want to generate an RTM report by providing the high-level document and RTM file paths, so that I can view the report with the RTM With Text option on a new screen.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to view the XML version of the RTM.

user_story: As a user, I want to view a list of automatically generated reports after completing a trace or assess process, so that I can save or modify the report design as needed.
true_user_story: After a project has completed either a trace or an assess process, then the user will need the capability to review the final results of the process in an easy to read format other than the XML file format that is automatically generated throughout the process.  The GENERATE REPORT function will provide the user a list of reports that the user may view.  The user will have the option to save the generated report or modify the design of the report. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save the RTM report from the Action menu with options for RTM and RTM With Text, so that I can access and review the required reports efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “RTM report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save reports such as 'Parents with no children', 'Children with no parents', and 'RTM report' from the Action menu, so that I can easily access and analyze specific relationship data as needed.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parent with no children report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save reports such as 'Parents with no children with text', 'Children with no parents', and 'Not satisfied requirements', so that I can easily review and document specific family and requirement data for analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Parents with no children with text report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save various relationship and requirements traceability reports, so that I can easily review and document system requirements and their relationships.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save reports such as 'Parents with no children with text', 'Children with no parents', and 'Not satisfied requirements', so that I can easily review and document specific family and requirement data for analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Children with no parents with text report”

user_story: As a user, I want to generate and save a 'Not satisfied requirements with text report,' so that I can document and review all requirements that have not been satisfied for further analysis and action steps in my project workflow.  This helps ensure traceability and accountability in requirements management processes.  I also want to access this report via a dedicated screen for clarity and ease of use.  Additionally, I want to filter and generate reports for 'Children with no parents', so that I
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements report”

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to generate and save a 'Not satisfied requirements with text' report from the Action menu, so that I can document and review unmet requirements efficiently.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to generate and save the “Not satisfied requirements with text report”

user_story: As a user, I want to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links, so that I can focus on relevant information and avoid being overwhelmed by unnecessary details.
true_user_story: “The user shall be able to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to choose whether the filter applies to a selected high level document element (LOCAL) or to all high level document elements (GLOBAL), so that I can control the scope of the filtering process according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate whether the filter process applies to the selected high level document element (LOCAL) or to all high level document elements (GLOBAL).

user_story: As a user, I want to filter the list of low-level requirements by setting a weight cutoff between 0 and 1.0, so that I can focus on the most relevant requirements for my analysis.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to filter the list of low-level requirements by setting a cutoff point for the weight between 0 and 1.0.

user_story: As a user, I want to select the 'Show Top' option and enter a number to display the top N elements, so that I can easily view the most relevant items based on my criteria.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to designate that the top N elements to be displayed.

user_story: As a user, I want to activate a 'Kill Hidden Links' function to remove all elements not currently displayed, so that my workspace is cleaner and only shows relevant information.
true_user_story: The user shall be able activate a KILL HIDDEN Links function to remove all elements not currently displayed.

user_story: As a user, I want to view and sort low-level requirements by Requirement ID and Weights, so that I can easily organize and analyze the requirements according to my needs.
true_user_story: The user will need the ability to view the text of low-level requirements.  The DISPLAY function will provide three different sort options to view the text of low-level requirements.  The user can sort the low-level requirements based upon the weight of linking relationships, original document order, or by requirement id. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to sort low level documents by Requirement ID, Weights, or Document Order, so that I can easily organize and find relevant information.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Requirement ID”

user_story: As a user, I want to sort low level documents by Requirement ID, Weights, or Document Order, so that I can easily organize and find documents based on my preferred criteria.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Weights”

user_story: As a user, I want to sort low level documents by Requirement ID, Weights, or Document Order, so that I can easily organize and find documents based on my preferred criteria.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to sort the low level documents by “Document Order”

user_story: As a user, I want to switch between 'Trace' and 'Assess' modes in RETRO, so that I can verify tracing accuracy and completeness and improve processes based on feedback.
true_user_story: Since RETRO’s tracing and assessing modes are based upon algorithms that will allow processes to be improved as the system receives FEEDBACK from the user.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to quickly remove low-level requirements with a status of 'No Link', so that I can efficiently clean up unlinked requirements from my project.
true_user_story: The user shall select a low level requirement and identify the link status from the following list: Link No Link Default

user_story: As a user, I want to load and work on multiple projects at the same time, so that I can efficiently manage and continue work on several ongoing projects without interruption.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to load multiple projects at the same time.

user_story: As a user, I want to open and work with multiple projects at the same time, so that I can efficiently manage and switch between different tasks without closing the RETRO application.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall allow multiple projects to be open at the same time

user_story: As a user, I want to import projects into the system, so that I can quickly create new projects and have them placed in Trace mode for further processing.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to import projects.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to prompt me for file paths for high-level and low-level requirements, so that I can easily provide the necessary input files for processing.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a mechanism to convert existing input text files into RETRO file format.  (Not implemented.)

user_story: As a user, I want to interact with the RETRO System through a user-friendly GUI interface, so that I can easily view and manage individual requirements without confusion or errors.
true_user_story: The user shall use a GUI INTERFACE to work with RETRO.

user_story: As a user, I want to interact with the RETRO System through a well-designed GUI interface, so that I can easily access its features without technical difficulties.
true_user_story: The RETRO System shall provide a GUI mechanism for the user to interact with the RETRO System.

user_story: As a user, I want to enter a keyword into a search box to find low-level requirements, so that I can quickly locate the specific requirement I need.
true_user_story: The user is provided the capability via BROWSE mode to manually link low-level requirements to high-level requirement. In order to help the user to quickly locate the desired low-level requirement or high-level requirement, a KEYWORD SEARCH function is offered to the user. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to enter a keyword into a search box to find matching low-level or high-level requirements, so that I can quickly locate the specific requirement I need.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within low-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a user, I want to enter a keyword into a search box to find matching high-level or low-level requirements, so that I can quickly locate the specific requirement I need.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to enter a keyword into a search text box to search within high-level requirements and the system shall return matches.

user_story: As a user, I want to manually delete recommended links in RETRO's BROWSE mode, so that I can ensure only accurate and relevant links are maintained.
true_user_story: There will be times when the user will not agree with RETRO’s list of candidate links.  Therefore, it is important to provide a mechanism for the user to manually change, create, or delete links between low and high level requirements.  BROWSE mode is the mechanism within RETRO that enables the user to perform MANUAL LINKING/TRACING. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to limit the display of low-level requirements or candidate requirement links, so that I can focus on relevant information and reduce clutter when reviewing requirement relationships.
true_user_story: The user shall be able to create and remove low-level requirements with high-level requirements.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to generate traces for all requirements or selected requirements, so that I can efficiently link low-level requirements to high-level ones and ensure complete coverage.
true_user_story: One of the purposes of RETRO is to aid the user in linking low-level requirements with high-level requirements.  TRACING functionality is the component of RETRO that automatically generates a list of candidate links of high to low-level requirements.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to generate traces for all requirements or selected requirements in RETRO, so that I can efficiently evaluate candidate links between high and low-level requirements.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on all requirements.

user_story: As a requirements analyst, I want to generate traces for all or selected requirements in RETRO, so that I can efficiently evaluate candidate links between high and low-level requirements.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to generate a trace on a given requirement(s).

user_story: As a project manager, I want to mark the tracing process as completed and prevent further changes, so that the project status is clearly finalized and traceability is maintained.
true_user_story: After the TRACING function is performed, then the results of the TRACING process will need to be made available to the user to view.  The TRACING RESULTS functionality will display a list of all candidate links of high to low-level requirements based on any given filtering criteria.  The user will be allowed to provide feedback on the TRACING RESULTS in order to improve future TRACING.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to display a list of candidate links with filtering and feedback options, so that I can efficiently review and manage traceability links according to my satisfaction.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display the list of candidate links based on the tracing and permit feedback, filtering, and display functionality.

user_story: As a user, I want to save the current trace result, so that I can preserve my progress and refer back to it later.
true_user_story: The SAVE TRACE function shall allow the user to save the trace result.

user_story: As a user, I want to generate a trace on all requirements or selected requirements and save the current status of the trace, so that I can track and manage requirement coverage efficiently in RETRO.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism to save the current status of trace.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to complete the trace process and mark the project as finished, so that no further changes can be made and the final tracing results are saved securely.
true_user_story: The COMPLETING TRACE function shall allow user to finish the tracing process.

user_story: As a project team member, I want RETRO to save all current data and the project when closing the current project, so that no work is lost and I can safely exit or switch projects.
true_user_story: RETRO shall mark the project completed and not permit further changes.

user_story: As a user, I want to assess the accuracy and completeness of tracing results, so that I can determine if the tracing was successful and provide feedback to improve future tracing.
true_user_story: The user needs a mechanism that will help to determine whether the TRACING was successfully performed based on accuracy and completeness.  An accuracy test tells the user that the candidate links provided are correctly related to the high-level requirement.  Completeness test tells the user that all possible links that are listed in the candidate list are included and no low-level requirements are missing. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to run an accuracy and completeness check by performing a full trace on requirements and comparing RETRO IR method results to the RTM, so that I can identify and improve any gaps in the requirements traceability.
true_user_story: RETRO shall provide a mechanism for the user to be able to run an ACCURACY & COMPLETENESS check by performing a full trace on the requirements and comparing the IR method results to the given RTM. [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to see a list of recommendations generated by RETRO's tracing process while in ASSESS mode, so that I can review and provide feedback on the candidate requirement links.
true_user_story: “The [user] will need to have a list of recommendations based on RETRO’s TRACING process.  SHOW RECOMMENDATION will display to the [user] a list of recommendation while in ASSESS mode.  The [user] will have the capability to provide FEEDBACK to the RETRO system on whether or not the [user] agrees with RETRO’s assessment of the linking between the low-level requirements to the high-level requirements.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want to display or hide the recommendations list in ASSESS mode, so that I can review and provide feedback on RETRO's tracing process as needed.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display/hide recommendations at the user’s request.

user_story: As a project manager, I want to see the satisfaction status (Satisfied, Not Satisfied, Partially Satisfied) of high-level requirements based on linked low-level requirements, so that I can assess project progress and requirement fulfillment.
true_user_story: Satisfaction function shows how satisfied the high-level requirement is with the given set of low-level requirements. [4] The Satisfaction Analysis function is checking the level of percentage for a satisfaction of the linked result with the high and low level input files. Once final output is agreed, the list of high level requirement is updated with options like “Satisfied”, “Not Satisfied” and “Partially Satisfied”. The status of high level requirement is color coded. Unsatisfied result can be repeated until all the high levels has been satisfied stage.

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to display the percentage level of satisfaction for each candidate link, so that I can quickly assess the quality of the links and make informed decisions.
true_user_story: RETRO shall display to the user the percentage level of link. [1]

user_story: As a user in ASSESS mode, I want to remove all low-level requirements with no links to high-level requirements, so that I can quickly clean up unlinked requirements and maintain traceability.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism to allow the removal of all low-level requirements that are not linked to a high-level requirement.  REMOVE ALL NO LINKS function will provide an easy way for the user to quickly remove low-level requirements with status of “No Link”.

user_story: As a user, I want to remove all links marked as 'No Link' in RETRO, so that I can efficiently clean up unnecessary or invalid links from my requirements traceability matrix.
true_user_story: RETRO shall remove all links marked as “No Link”

user_story: As a user in ASSESS mode, I want to be able to select and delete recommended links that are incorrect, so that I can ensure the accuracy of requirement traceability.
true_user_story: While in ASSESS mode, the user will need a mechanism that will allow the deletion of recommended links that the user feels are not correctly linked.

user_story: As a user, I want to be able to select and delete recommended links, as well as have all links marked as 'No Link' automatically removed, so that I can efficiently manage and clean up unnecessary or invalid links in the system.
true_user_story: RETRO shall permit the user to select a recommended link and delete it.

user_story: As a user, I want each candidate link to be color-coded based on its satisfaction status, so that I can quickly and easily identify the satisfaction level of each link at a glance.
true_user_story: “In order to help the user to quickly identify the level of satisfaction of each candidate link, RETRO will need to provide a visual aid.  COLOR-CODED SATISFACTION STATUS function will color code each candidate link based on its satisfaction level.   The various colors will be a visual aid to the user to help quickly identify each links satisfaction level.” [1]

user_story: As a user, I want RETRO to color-code the satisfaction status of each candidate link, so that I can quickly and easily identify the satisfaction level of each link at a glance.
true_user_story: RETRO shall COLOR-CODE THE SATISFACTION STATUS of each given candidate link.
